title: FlashSpec: Adaptive Speculative Decoding with Online Bandit Draft Selection and Triton-Optimised Verification
description: FlashSpec is an open-source adaptive speculative-decoding inference engine for large language models. It implements GPU-native token verification via a Triton kernel with O(1) SRAM usage in vocabulary size, and frames draft-model selection as a K-armed bandit problem solved online via UCB1 or Thompson sampling with a provable O(sqrt(K T log T)) regret bound. The output distribution is provably identical to standard autoregressive sampling from the target model, verified continuously in CI via a Kolmogorov-Smirnov test (alpha=0.01, N=10,000).
notes: Generated for Zenodo archival via GitHub release integration. To activate: (1) sign in to zenodo.org with GitHub, (2) enable the FlashSpec repository under Settings > GitHub, (3) create a new GitHub Release (e.g. v0.1.0) — Zenodo will automatically mint a DOI and archive a snapshot of the repository at that tag. Update CITATION.cff and README.md with the resulting DOI badge afterward.
